events.title: Veld, Gold, and the Open Frontier
events.scene: The frontier is raw with opportunity — farmland to be claimed, mineral rights to be staked, contracts to be won as the colony pushes inland. The {surname} line can root itself in the soil, chase the glitter under it, or build the machines the diggings cannot do without. The continent rewards nerve and punishes hesitation.
events.researchNote: Place-specific Afrikaner/Cape origins beat (EX-2): the late-19th-c. mineral frontier (gold, diamonds) and farm-to-fortune mobility. Generic to any south_africa founding.

choices.text: Stake mineral claims — high risk, transformative capital if they pay
choices.outcome: You gamble on a claim others passed over. When it pays, the {surname} line has capital while a hundred cautious rivals have only dust.

choices.text: Build a farm-and-trade empire rooted in the land
choices.outcome: You build on soil and steady trade rather than the gamble. The {surname} name becomes a fixture of the district, slow and unshakeable.

choices.text: Master the engineering the diggings cannot run without
choices.outcome: You sell the colony its machines and its know-how. The {surname} line earns from every claim without staking one — and a taste for invention takes root.

events.title: War Comes to the Veld
events.scene: The colony tears itself apart — farms burned, families herded into camps, loyalties tested at gunpoint. The {surname} line must decide where it stands when neighbours become enemies and the victor's favour will shape a generation of fortunes.
events.researchNote: The South African War (1899–1902) as the defining rupture for Cape/Afrikaner families — collaboration, resistance, or survival. Place-specific.

choices.text: Read the wind and align with the side that will win
choices.outcome: You back the winning side before it has won. The {surname} line emerges from the ashes richer and quietly despised — a trade many would make.

choices.text: Hold with your own people, whatever it costs
choices.outcome: You stand with your kin through the worst of it. The {surname} name carries a hard-won honour that no fortune could have bought.

events.title: A Restlessness in the Blood
events.scene: The frontier is closing, the easy claims are gone, and the next generation of the {surname} line stares at horizons the colony can no longer offer. Some families dig deeper roots; others breed children who will not stay where they were born. What does the {surname} line teach its young?
events.researchNote: The Cape diaspora impulse — a restless, outward-looking heir who eventually carries the line to richer countries. Sets a frontier-spirit flag the line carries forward.

choices.text: Raise heirs who measure the world by what lies past the horizon
choices.outcome: Your children grow up certain the colony is too small for them. The {surname} line will one day plant itself in a far larger country.

choices.text: Teach the young that the land they hold is worth holding
choices.outcome: The {surname} line digs in. Whatever the wider world offers, the family's strength stays bound to the veld it knows.
